meaning_english: To follow or obey a rule, law, or decision. Synonyms: Comply with, Adhere to.
To explain the reason for something. Synonyms: Explain, Justify.
To request something. Synonyms: Request, Petition.
To withdraw from a commitment or promise. Synonyms: Withdraw, Retreat.
To be patient with someone. Synonyms: Tolerate, Endure.
To extinguish a flame by blowing. Synonyms: Extinguish, Quench.
To stop functioning (machinery) or to lose emotional control. Synonyms: Fail, Collapse.
To enter a building by force. Synonyms: Intrude, Burgle.
To raise a child or to mention a topic. Synonyms: Rear, Nurture, Mention.
To cancel something that was planned. Synonyms: Cancel, Abandon.
To visit someone briefly. Synonyms: Visit, Drop by.
To continue doing something. Synonyms: Continue, Proceed.
To examine something or leave a hotel. Synonyms: Inspect, Investigate.
To find or meet something by chance. Synonyms: Encounter, Run into.
To reduce the amount or consumption. Synonyms: Reduce, Curtail.
To trade or sell a particular product. Synonyms: Trade, Market.
To have an argument and stop being friends. Synonyms: Quarrel, Clash.
To have a friendly relationship. Synonyms: Be friendly, Harmonize.
To stop trying or quit a habit. Synonyms: Quit, Surrender.
To experience a difficulty or to examine something. Synonyms: Experience, Examine.
To wait for a short time or grip tightly. Synonyms: Wait, Pause.
To continue at the same speed or level. Synonyms: Maintain, Sustain.
To take care of someone or something. Synonyms: Tend, Guard.
To try to find something. Synonyms: Search, Seek.
To investigate the facts of a situation. Synonyms: Investigate, Probe.
To delay doing something until a later time. Synonyms: Postpone, Defer.
To tolerate an unpleasant situation or person. Synonyms: Tolerate, Endure.
To finish the supply of something. Synonyms: Exhaust, Use up.
To leave the ground (plane) or remove clothing. Synonyms: Depart, Lift off.
To refuse an offer or request. Synonyms: Reject, Decline.
